---
title: "Datasets (regdatasets)"
---

## Overview

All datasets used in {{< var course.number >}} are bundled in the **regdatasets** R package (v0.2.0). This package provides {{< var materials.datasets >}} curated educational datasets drawn from published research in education, psychology, and the social sciences. Each dataset is cleaned, documented, and ready for analysis.

{{< fa database >}} [**regdatasets Package Documentation**]({{< var books.regdatasets >}}){target="_blank"}


## Installation

Install the package from GitHub:

```r
# Install remotes if you haven't already
install.packages("remotes")

# Install regdatasets
remotes::install_github("joonho112/regdatasets")
```

::: {.callout-tip}
## Updating the package
To get the latest version, simply re-run the `install_github()` command above. This will overwrite the existing installation.
:::


## Basic Usage

```r
# Load the package
library(regdatasets)

# Load a specific dataset
data(gcse)

# Explore the dataset
glimpse(gcse)
?gcse          # View documentation
```


## Key Datasets Used in This Course

The table below lists the primary datasets referenced in homework assignments, labs, and lecture examples.

| Dataset | Description | Used In |
|---------|-------------|---------|
| `gcse` | GCSE exam scores with school-level and student-level predictors | Chapter 1 (Simple Regression) |
| `teacher_pay` | Teacher salary data across U.S. states | Chapter 2 (Simple Regression) |
| `tutoring` | Randomized tutoring experiment with pre/post scores | Chapter 4 (ANCOVA) |
| `star` | Tennessee STAR class-size experiment | Chapter 5 (One-Way ANOVA) |
| `eeo_school` | Coleman Report school-level achievement data | Chapter 6 (Multiple Regression) |
| `salary_gender` | Faculty salary data with gender and rank | Chapter 7 (Interactions) |
| `campus_crime` | Campus crime rates and institutional characteristics | Chapter 8 (Nonlinear Relationships) |
| `pisa2000` | PISA 2000 international assessment data | Chapter 9 (Model Building) |
| `titanic` | Titanic passenger survival data | Chapter 10 (Simple Logistic Regression) |
| `berk_sub` | Berkeley admissions subset (Simpson's paradox) | Chapter 11 (Multiple Logistic Regression) |
| `contraception` | Contraceptive use in Bangladesh (binary/ordinal outcome) | Chapters 13--14 (GLM & Ordinal) |

::: {.callout-note}
## Additional datasets
The `regdatasets` package contains {{< var materials.datasets >}} datasets in total. Many additional datasets appear in the Lab Manual and supplementary exercises. Run `data(package = "regdatasets")` to see the full list.
:::


## Exploring Available Datasets

To see all datasets in the package:

```r
# List all datasets
data(package = "regdatasets")

# Get help for a specific dataset
?star
?grad_admit
```

Each dataset includes full documentation describing the variables, their types, and the original source.


## Using Datasets in Your Final Project

You are welcome to use any dataset from the `regdatasets` package for your final project. You may also use an external dataset relevant to your research interests. See the [Project Guide]({{< var books.project_guide >}}){target="_blank"} for dataset requirements and approval procedures.
